content.text: This example shows how to retrieve the most recent entries while implementing a reusable pagination logic. By using constants for pageLength, you keep your query logic clean and easy to maintain.

Use this approach when you want to retrieve entries that are linked to one or more specific items. This is particularly useful for features like "Filtering by Category" or "View all posts by these Authors."

If you need to find entries that contain all specified references simultaneously, you can map over your IDs using an and operator. This ensures every single link in your list must be present for a match.

This pattern demonstrates how to find related content by comparing shared references. First, we retrieve the current entry to extract its linked metadata. Then, we use those IDs to query other entries of the same type, ensuring the current item is excluded from the results to provide a clean list of recommendations.
In the previous example we hardcoded the locale and url, but it's very likely that your framework supports some sort of routing params:
